core_takeaway: Agent_1 used first position to frame Seed B and make Pencil-to-Agent_1 the shared baseline, but that agenda-setting did not become a broader payoff edge. The coalition redirected Apple to Agent_3, anchored Quill with Agent_4, and preserved Stone for Agent_5, leaving Agent_1 and Agent_5 tied at 26.1.
why_or_counterexample: There is clear evidence of first-speaker agenda-setting around Pencil, but the outcome is a tie with the last speaker and the process evidence shows coalition balance, explicit 2/3 commitments, and Agent_2's holdout mattered more than Agent_1's position. Agent_5, speaking last, still secured Stone for the same discounted utility as Agent_1.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened with Seed B and a Pencil/Apple target. Other agents accepted Pencil-to-Agent_1 as the baseline, but repeatedly moved Apple to Agent_3, Quill to Agent_4, Jewel to Agent_2, and Stone to Agent_5.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private notes converged on the same Round 2/3 baseline: keep Pencil with Agent_1, anchor Quill with Agent_4, send Apple to Agent_3, and use Jewel/Stone for balance. Agent_1 still wanted Apple, but its own private strategy prepared to defend the public baseline rather than force Apple.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: All formal proposals in both rounds used the identical allocation. Round 1 failed unanimity, but Round 2 reached unanimous acceptance, with the system selecting Proposal #5 despite all proposals being substantively the same.
phase_notes.private_voting: Round 1 failed because Agent_2 rejected every identical proposal to pursue Quill rather than settle for Jewel. In Round 2 Agent_2 accepted Jewel to preserve the public Quill anchor and avoid deadlock; Agent_1 and Agent_5 each accepted utilities of 26.1.
phase_notes.reflection: Reflections diagnosed the blocker as missing explicit 2/3 commitments, especially around Quill and Apple, not as a problem solved by Agent_1's positional leverage. The recommended fix was a public coalition charter with predefined backups.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker set the initial Seed B frame
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's opening created a durable public frame: Pencil stayed with Agent_1 across the discussion, proposals, and reflections. This is the main process evidence for a first-position benefit, but it covered only one item.

evidence.quote: Seed B (stepwise): I take Pencil now.
Nice framing, Agent_1. I can align with Seed B
All proposals keep Pencil with Agent_1 in Round 1

mechanisms.theme: Coalition balance redirected Agent_1's Apple ambition
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 initially wanted Pencil and Apple, but the coalition settled on Apple for Agent_3 and Stone for Agent_5. This counterbalances the opening advantage and explains why Agent_1 did not beat the last speaker.

evidence.quote: secure Pencil and Apple for myself
Apple to Agent_3 (me); Quill to Agent_4; Jewel to Agent_2; Stone to Agent_5
gives me Stone (my top mid-tier) by Round 2/3

mechanisms.explanation: Once proposals were formalized, every agent proposed the same allocation. The final accepted proposal was Proposal #5, showing that acceptance reflected shared terms rather than a privilege for Agent_1's proposal.

mechanisms.theme: Agent_2's Quill holdout shaped the process more than first position
mechanisms.explanation: Round 1 failed because Agent_2 rejected the identical allocation to pursue Quill. Round 2 succeeded when Agent_2 accepted Jewel as part of a public Quill-to-Agent_4 anchor, so the decisive shift was satisfying the holdout's process concerns.

evidence.quote: I receive Jewel (9) here. I value Quill (36) most
Agent_2’s strong preference to land Quill themselves creates a hard bottleneck
Accepting keeps Pencil momentum for Agent_1 and prevents deadlock

mechanisms.theme: Explicit public commitments produced a tie, not a first-position win
mechanisms.explanation: The successful Round 2 strategy was to lock in a public timing package for all agents. In voting, Agent_1 and Agent_5 each accepted the same discounted utility, matching the final first-minus-last value of zero.
